LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT RACHEL WHETSTONE'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND FOR AN ORDER STAYING THE ACTION. *** REDACTED VERSION *** IS GRANTED AND THIS CASE IS STAYED PENDING CONCLUSION OF THE ARBITRATION PROCEEDINGS. UNLIKE BRODKE V. ALPHATEC SPINE INC. (2008) 160 CAL.APP.4TH 1569, 1574, MS. WHETSTONE HAS AFFIRMATIVELY ASSERTED THAT SHE IS A SIGNATORY TO AN EXISTING VALID WRITTEN AGREEMENT CONTAINING AN ARBITRATION CLAUSE. SINCE PLAINTIFF ERIC ALEXANDER'S COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ENFORCE THE TERMS OF THIS WRITTEN AGREEMENT AS A THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY, MR. ALEXANDER IS EQUITABLY ESTOPPED FROM REPUDIATING THE AGREEMENT'S ARBITRATION CLAUSE AND FROM AVOIDING ARBITRATION OF HIS BREACH OF CONTRACT CLAIM THAT IS WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE. (JSM TUSCANY, LLC V. SUPERIOR COURT (2011) 193 CAL.APP.4TH 1222, 1239-1240 (WHEN A "PLAINTIFF IS SUING ON A CONTRACT-ON THE BASIS THAT, EVEN THOUGH THE PLAINTIFF WAS NOT A PARTY TO THE CONTRACT, THE PLAINTIFF IS NONETHELESS ENTITLED TO RECOVER FOR ITS BREACH, THE PLAINTIFF SHOULD BE EQUITABLY ESTOPPED FROM REPUDIATING THE CONTRACT'S ARBITRATION CLAUSE . . . ADDITIONALLY, A NONSIGNATORY CAN BE COMPELLED TO ARBITRATE WHEN IT IS SUING AS A THIRD-PARTY BENEFICIARY OF THE CONTRACT CONTAINING THE ARBITRATION CLAUSE.")). ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: W. TRUPEK; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM (302/HEK)

Defendant Rachel Whetstone'S Motion To Compel Arbitration And For An Order Staying The Action. *** Redacted Version ***
Off Calendar Dec-19-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Vicki Parker, Esq., Quinn Emanuel, Telephone 415-875-6503, appearing for Plaintiff;
John M. Potter, Esq., Quinn Emanuel, Telephone 415-875-6503, appearing for Plaintiff;
Angela C. Agrusa, Esq., DLP Piper, Telephone 310-500-3591, appearing for Defendant Rachel Whetstone;
Tamany Vinson Bentz, Esq., DLP Piper, Telephone 310-500-3591, appearing for Defendant Rachel Whetstone;

RULING - ARGUED: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Defendant Rachel Whetstone's motion to compel arbitration is granted and this case is stayed pending conclusion of the arbitration proceedings. Unlike Brodke v. Alphatec Spine Inc. (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1569, 1574, Ms. Whetstone has affirmatively asserted that she is a signatory to an existing valid written agreement containing an arbitration clause. Since plaintiff Eric Alexander's complaint seeks to enforce the terms of this written agreement as a third party beneficiary, Mr. Alexander is equitably estopped from repudiating the agreement's arbitration clause and from avoiding arbitration of his breach of contract claim that is within the scope of the arbitration clause. (JSM Tuscany, LLC v. Superior Court (2011) 193 Cal.App.4th 1222, 1239-1240 (when a "plaintiff is suing on a contract-on the basis that, even though the plaintiff was not a party to the contract, the plaintiff is nonetheless entitled to recover for its breach, the plaintiff should be equitably estopped from repudiating the contract's arbitration clause . . . Additionally, a nonsignatory can be compelled to arbitrate when it is suing as a third-party beneficiary of the contract containing the arbitration clause.")). Order is signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/HEK)
